message:
fix(crypto): chain root-cause errors at encrypt.rs crypto sites [coding_agent_session_search-b64fe]

Bead b64fe (partial slice): pre-fix, six .map_err(|_| ...) sites in
src/pages/encrypt.rs (lines 206, 234, 573, 619, 677, 680) discarded
the underlying error type and replaced it with generic messages like
"Decryption failed for chunk N" or "Key unwrapping failed". An
operator staring at one of these messages had no way to distinguish
wrong password from corrupted key slot from hardware failure from a
downstream layer regression masquerading as a crypto error.

What landed (4 high-value sites in src/pages/encrypt.rs):
- Line 206 (add_password_slot): u8::try_from key-slot count overflow
  now chains the slot count + the underlying TryFromIntError.
- Line 234 (add_recovery_slot): same chain pattern as line 206.
- Line 573 (chunk decryption loop): chains the aead error +
  ciphertext length so operators can correlate with which chunk and
  how big the ciphertext was. Comment notes the aead Display impl
  remains opaque about MAC vs auth-tag verification (timing-attack
  hardening) — the chain doesn't leak that, but the source error
  type IS preserved for debug-mode inspection.
- Line 619 (derive_kek_hkdf length check): captures actual_len BEFORE
  the .try_into() consumes the Vec so the message can show "expected
  32, got N".
- Line 680 (unwrap_key): chains the aead error + slot id + wrapped /
  nonce / aad lengths so operators can correlate with which slot they
  were attempting and sanity-check the inputs.

Plus the related line 615 hkdf_extract_expand .map_err now includes
the call site context ("HKDF extract+expand failed for recovery
secret KEK: {e}") instead of bare {e}.

Two new regression gates:
- unwrap_key_chains_aead_source_error_into_diagnostic_message wraps
  a real DEK, tampers the auth tag, calls unwrap_key, and asserts the
  rendered error contains: slot id, wrapped-ciphertext length,
  nonce length, the ":" source-separator, AND the legacy
  "Key unwrapping failed" prefix (so existing operator runbooks /
  grep patterns still match).
- derive_kek_hkdf_error_message_pins_actual_kek_length codifies the
  expected message shape ("expected 32, got 16") so a future refactor
  that drops actual_len fails the assertion.

14/14 lib pages::encrypt tests green via
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p2 cargo test
--lib pages::encrypt.

Scope note: the bead lists 13 sites across encrypt.rs (6) AND
key_management.rs (7). This commit fixes encrypt.rs only.
key_management.rs is forbidden under the marching orders for this
pane (src/pages/key_management.rs is on the do-not-touch list); the
remaining 7 sites belong to a follow-up bead claimed by another pane.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
